name: Serra Calderona Natural Park - Circular to Pico del Águila from Olocau
name_original: Parque Natural de la Serra Calderona — Circular al Pico del Águila desde Olocau
region_intro: Mountain biking around Olocau offers a diverse network of trails within the Sierra Calderona Natural Park, a prominent "green lung" near Valencia. The region features extensive forests, rocky mountains, and varied terrain suitable for different skill levels. Riders can explore geological formations, ravines, and historical sites while navigating the area's extensive trail system. This landscape provides a mix of challenging ascents and technical descents, alongside more moderate paths. Best mountain bike trails around Olocau The most popular mountain bike trail is Serra Calderona Natural Park - Circular to Pico del Águila from Olocau, a 20.1 miles (32.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 41 minutes to complete. This difficult route leads to Pico del Águila, offering panoramic views. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Mirador de l'Abella – Font de l'Abella loop from Petromar, a moderate 12.6 miles (20.4 km) path. This route features viewpoints and natural springs within a varied landscape. Local mountain bikers also love the Font de la Cava – Drop Spring loop from Olocau, a 12.9 miles (20.7 km) trail leading through the natural park's diverse terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 39 minutes. Mountain biking around Olocau is defined by the Sierra Calderona Natural Park's extensive forests, rocky mountain features, and ancient ravines. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Olocau are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.95 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Olocau's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Olocau?
A: Olocau, situated within the Sierra Calderona Natural Park, offers a wide network of mountain bike trails. There are over 150 cycling routes in the area, with 19 specifically curated mountain bike tours available on komoot, catering to various skill levels.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Olocau?
A: Yes, Olocau has mountain bike trails suitable for beginners. While many routes offer challenging ascents, there are 4 easy-rated tours available on komoot, alongside 11 moderate options, providing a good range for those new to mountain biking or looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What are some of the most challenging mountain bike routes around Olocau?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Olocau offers several difficult routes. A notable one is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e909538377/serra-calderona-natural-park-circular-to-pico-del-aguila-from-olocau" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra Calderona Natural Park - Circular to Pico del Águila from Olocau&lt;/a&gt;, a 32.4 km trail with a significant 990-meter elevation gain, leading to the impressive Pico del Águila viewpoint. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10378485" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font de la Cava – Drop Spring loop from Olocau&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 20.7 km with over 700 meters of climbing.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Olocau?
A: Absolutely. Many of the mountain bike trails in Olocau are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40369523" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de l'Abella – Font de l'Abella loop from Petromar&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14841162" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Carrasqueta – Old Arquet Aqueduct loop from Olocau&lt;/a&gt; offers a longer, moderate circular experience.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails in Olocau?
A: While the region is known for its challenging terrain, there are multi-use paths and easier sections within the Sierra Calderona Natural Park that can be suitable for families. Look for routes designated as 'easy' on komoot, which typically have less elevation gain and smoother surfaces, though specific 'family-friendly' designations may vary.

Q: Which mountain bike trails offer the best panoramic views in Olocau?
A: For breathtaking views, consider routes that ascend to prominent peaks or viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e909538377/serra-calderona-natural-park-circular-to-pico-del-aguila-from-olocau" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Águila&lt;/a&gt;, at 878 meters, offers one of the most impressive natural viewpoints in Valencia. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839906" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Olocau and the Cliffs of Cova del Cavall&lt;/a&gt; is another highlight providing excellent vistas.

Q: What historical or natural attractions can I see along the mountain bike trails in Olocau?
A: Olocau's trails are rich with natural and historical sights. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4241420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco del Carraixet&lt;/a&gt;, known for its century-old carob trees. Historical sites include the remains of the 11th-century Castillo del Real and the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14841162" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Arquet Aqueduct&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features unique geological formations, including enormous breccias and conglomerates.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Olocau?
A: The mountain bike trails in Olocau are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 3.95 stars from over 20 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the extensive forests of the Sierra Calderona Natural Park, and the mix of challenging ascents and technical descents available.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Olocau?
A: Olocau, with its Mediterranean climate, generally offers good mountain biking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, providing pleasant temperatures and lush scenery. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during these months to avoid the peak heat.

Q: Are there any other interesting natural features to explore besides the trails?
A: Beyond the trails, the Sierra Calderona Natural Park itself is a significant natural feature, known as a 'green lung' near Valencia. You can explore various geological formations, including ancient breccias and conglomerates, and discover diverse Mediterranean vegetation, including cork oak species. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10377173" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Pedra Grossa – Coka en Roka Trail loop from Urbanització Pedralbilla&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate ride through some of these natural landscapes.

Q: Is parking available near the mountain bike trailheads in Olocau?
A: As Olocau serves as a common starting point for many routes within the Sierra Calderona Natural Park, you can typically find parking options within or on the outskirts of the town. Specific parking availability may vary by trailhead, but general access to the park is well-established for visitors.
tips: Top of Pico del Águila at 878m above sea level with incredible 360-degree views of Gátova, the Sierra de Espadan, the Alt Palancia Valley and the Sierra Calderona. The access road begins in Gatova with sections of somewhat steep slope but with a concrete surface, so it can be traveled without problems in any weather condition. When you arrive at the forest hut, all you have to do is take a few minutes to catch your breath and appreciate the beautiful views!

The history goes back to its construction in 1610 by the doctor surnamed Tristany who donated the farmhouse to the Carthusian monks. Until a few years ago it was part of the Valencia Hikers Center. It is currently abandoned and can be used as a free shelter for some bikepacking outing.

Waterfalls in La Pedralbilla a few meters from the Font de la Gota. They are located on the side of the road in a very picturesque place surrounded by vegetation. A nice place to rest and in summer even be able to cool off!
